signals.title: AUD/JPY — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: AUD/JPY at 114.578 — AUD outperformance hits critical threshold with a +0.976% weekly average across G8 peers, while JPY remains the primary laggard at -0.592%. The 1.568% spread is reinforced by a +360bp RBA-BoJ carry advantage (RBA 4.35% vs BoJ 0.75%). Despite BoJ hike bias, the bear steepener regime in US yields (2s10s +53.9bp) and normal volatility (VIX 18.0) sustain carry-seeking flows into AUD. Watch for RBA meeting on 16 Jun to confirm hiking trajectory.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Inflation Surprise + Yield Correlation Break
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.845 — US PPI surprise (6.0% YoY vs 4.9% est) reinforces higher-for-longer Fed outlook, lifting US 10Y yields to 4.489%. USD/JPY vs US 10Y correlation has broken to +0.541 (z=-2.23σ), indicating the pair is now hyper-sensitive to US terminal rate repricing rather than domestic JPY factors. Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75% (+300bp) remains the structural floor. Intervention risk rises if price tests 160.00; next BoJ meeting (mkt: 40% hike priced) determines if domestic policy can offset US yield pressure.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7261 — COT LF net long +58,994 (78.3% long) indicates heavy institutional backing, while retail sentiment is at an 88% short extreme. This institutional vs retail divergence at the top of a 1W +0.73% move suggests retail is unsuccessfully fading the trend. Risk of retail capitulation squeeze remains high as long as RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% maintains the -60bp AUD carry premium. Watch for LF long% exceeding 80% for signs of institutional exhaustion.

signals.title: GBP/USD — Geopolitical Risk + Leadership Crisis
signals.text: GBP/USD at 1.3515 — Sterling is under pressure following BoE Mann's warnings on UK geopolitical exposure and reports of a domestic leadership crisis. GBP COT LF net long +31,244 (65.3% long) represents a crowded long position vulnerable to liquidation if political instability persists. Fed 3.75% vs BoE 3.75% (0bp spread) offers no carry protection for GBP against USD strength. Price holding below 20-day EMA confirms bearish momentum; watch for BoE meeting on 18 Jun for policy shift.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5934 — COT LF net short -19,294 (18.5% long) shows specs are heavily short despite a hawkish RBNZ bias (Next meeting 27 May). This positioning-policy divergence creates short-squeeze risk if the RBNZ signals a hike. However, the +150bp carry advantage for USD (Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25%) currently supports the spec short thesis. Watch RBNZ decision on 27 May to trigger either a short unwind or a move toward 0.5850.
